description_long: This object is a cylindrical bottle stopper, most likely a natural cork, with a slightly bulged top and a rough, uneven surface. The top face is circular and shows some faint, possibly stamped markings or text, which are partially worn and faded. The body of the stopper is textured with visible grain and irregularities typical of cork material, and it appears to have some discoloration and darkened areas, possibly from contact with liquid or aging. The bottom edge is slightly jagged and not perfectly flat, suggesting it has been removed from a bottle. The overall color is a mix of light tan and brown, with some darker patches and stains. There are no visible seams, caps, or additional components, and the object appears solid and opaque.
description: A cylindrical, natural cork bottle stopper with a rough, textured tan and brown surface, faint markings on the top, and signs of wear and discoloration.
description_view_0: The top face of the cork is visible, showing faint, worn markings and a circular, slightly uneven surface.
description_view_1: The side of the cork is shown, highlighting the rough, grainy texture and some dark discoloration along the body.
description_view_2: Another side view reveals more of the cork's irregular surface and jagged bottom edge, with visible wear.
description_view_3: The cork is seen from an angle, displaying both the rounded top and the cylindrical body with its natural texture and color variations.
attribute_label_or_branding: Faint, partially worn markings or text on the top face, but not clearly legible.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible wear, discoloration, and some jaggedness on the bottom edge.
